tags.name: Opportunities
tags.description: Aggregated campaign data grouped by pool/vault/market. Query by ID, list with filters (chain, protocol, APR, TVL), get APR/TVL distribution bins, and aggregate statistics (min/max/sum).

tags.name: Campaigns
tags.description: Individual reward campaigns incentivizing specific DeFi actions. List/count campaigns, get campaign details by ID or creation transaction hash, retrieve user participation metrics, fetch linked parent/child campaigns, and generate reward distribution payloads for on-chain claiming.

tags.name: Protocols
tags.description: DeFi protocol registry. List all protocols, search by ID or name, count protocols, and access protocol metadata (icon, description, supported chains).

tags.name: Chains
tags.description: Blockchain network configurations. List all supported chains, get chain details by ID, and access RPC endpoints and block explorer links.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: User reward tracking by wallet address. Get total claimable rewards across all chains, retrieve detailed breakdowns by campaign and opportunity, filter by chain or claimable status, and check terms of service acceptance.

tags.name: Carousels
tags.description: Frontend promotional banners. List all carousels with ordering, get carousel by ID or slug, and preview carousel content.

tags.name: Programs
tags.description: Protocol incentive programs grouping multiple campaigns. List programs with filters, get program details by ID or slug, and view all associated campaigns.

tags.name: Templates
tags.description: Campaign templates for quick setup. Pre-defined campaign configurations that can be used to create new campaigns with minimal input. Includes config templates with fields to fill.

tags.name: Claims
tags.description: Claim tracking and status monitoring. Track on-chain claim events from Merkl distributors, monitor indexing progress by chain and distributor source, and check claim status across all supported chains.

This endpoint enables you to search for campaigns by providing specific criteria through query parameters.

Number of items returned by page

schema.description: Filter by unique database ID of the campaign resource
Filter campaigns created by a user who has a specific tag
Filter campaigns created by a user who is registered as a merkl creator

Filter by compute chain.
You can get the list of all supported chains by calling [GET /v4/chains](#tag/chains/GET/v4/chains/)

anyOf.description:
Filter by compute chain.
You can get the list of all supported chains by calling [GET /v4/chains](#tag/chains/GET/v4/chains/)

schema.description: Search amongst multiple values (token, protocols, tags, campaigns)

Filter by distribution chain.
You can get the list of all supported chains by calling [GET /v4/chains](#tag/chains/GET/v4/chains/)
Filter by type of campaign
Filter by type of campaign using an array. The `type` entry has priority.
Silo, Radiant, Morpho, Dolomite, Compound, Ajna and Euler campaigns have a subtype attribute you can filter on
Filter campaigns by campaignId. Some campaigns take place on different blockchains, one resource per chain will be returned, sharing the same campaignId
Filter campaigns by multiple campaignIds. The `campaignId` entry has priority over this one.
the mainParameter is what enables grouping campaigns per opportunity (it can be a random hash or an address like a Uniswap pool)
Filter by reward token symbol (e.g. USDC for campaigns rewarding in USDC)
Filter results by the reward token address. Use in combination with chainId for accuracy
Return campaigns rewarding in test tokens
Filter by token type. Use POINT to include point campaigns and PRETGE to include preTGE campaigns. By default every token is returned.
Return campaigns rewarding in points
Filter by opportunity. You can find opportunity ids by calling [GET /v4/opportunities](#tag/opportunities/GET/v4/opportunities)
A comma separeted list of status. Legal values are: `LIVE`, `PAST`, `SOON`
Returns campaigns starting *after* the given unix timestamp
Returns campaigns ending *after* the given unix timestamp
Include opportunity data. Will slow down the request. Default: false

items.description: Filter by distribution type. Legal values are: `FIX_REWARD`, `MAX_REWARD`, `DUTCH_AUCTION`. Default: no filter

anyOf.description: Return campaigns created after the given date. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

Return campaigns created before the given date. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

get.description:
**Retrieve Multiple Opportunities**

This endpoint enables you to search for opportunities by providing specific criteria through query parameters.

#### Request Examples ##### List opportunities by protocol name ```bash curl "https://api.merkl.xyz/v4/opportunities?name=Euler" ``` Returns all opportunities associated with the Euler protocol, including aggregate metrics. ##### Filter by tag ```bash curl "https://api.merkl.xyz/v4/opportunities?tags=zksync" ``` Returns opportunities tagged with "zksync" across all supported protocols.

schema.description: 0-indexed page number
Number of items returned by page

anyOf.description: Number of items returned by page

schema.description: Filter by name
Search amongst multiple values (token, protocols, tags, campaigns)
Search the opportunity linked to a given campaignId
Filter by creator slug

A comma separated list of chain ids. Example: ?chainId=1,42161
You can get the list of all supported chains by calling [GET /v4/chains](#tag/chains/GET/v4/chains/)
A comma seprated list actions. Legal values are: POOL, HOLD, DROP, LEND, BORROW, LONG, SHORT, SWAP, INVALID, STAKE
Filter by token type. Use POINT to include point campaigns and PRETGE to include preTGE campaigns. By default every token is returned.
Include opportunities with point campaigns
A comma separated list of Opportunity type
Filter by creator address
Filter by tag
Include opportunities with test campaigns
Minimum TVL threshold in USD
Maximum TVL threshold in USD
Minimum APR threshold
Maximum APR threshold
Minimum daily rewards threshold in USD

A comma separeted list of status. Legal values are: `LIVE`, `PAST`, `SOON`
Filter by identifier (mainParameter).
A comma separated list of opportunity ids. Example: ?id=5877322385708089533,1234567890
Include campaign data. Will slow down the request. Default: false
A comma separated list of token symbol. Use to filter by token
Filter opportunities that include at least one stablecoin token
Filter by opportunity that have at least 1 campaigns where the reward token has this symbol
Sort by `apr`, `tvl`, `rewards`, `maxDailyRewards`, last campaign creation date, campaign start times, or campaign end times
`asc` to sort in ascending order or `desc` to sort in descending order.
